title: ‘Zelenskyy Regime Core Exporter Of Instability’: Russia Reacts To NIA Arrest Of Ukrainians In India
description: Russia has reacted strongly to India’s arrest of six Ukrainian citizens and a US national by the NIA for alleged links to proscribed insurgent groups. Russian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Maria Zakharova said the individuals allegedly crossed from Mizoram into Myanmar, connected with local armed groups, supplied EU-made drones, and trained militants in drone assembly, repair, and electronic warfare. She criticised Ukraine for trying to conceal the incident and remaining silent on violations of India’s counter-terrorism laws, calling Kyiv a “core exporter of instability” involved in global arms black markets and regional conflicts. India’s MEA confirmed consular access requests have been received, with all seven detainees, including US citizen Matthew Van Dyke, remanded to 11-day NIA custody.
creator: timesofindia.com
keywords: nia custody india; ukrainian citizens india; russia reaction india; insurgent groups northeast india; toi; us citizen detained india; matthew van dyke; ukraine embassy india; toi bharat; india nia arrests; india counter terrorism law; drone supply india; mizoram myanmar border; electronic warfare training
rewritten_news:
‘Zelenskyy Regime Core Exporter Of Instability’: Russia Reacts To NIA Arrest Of Ukrainians In India

Russia has denounced India’s detention of six Ukrainian nationals and a US citizen by the NIA, which alleges their ties to banned insurgent organisations. According to Russian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Maria Zakharova, the suspects reportedly entered Myanmar from Mizoram, linked up with local militias, provided European‑made drones, and instructed fighters in drone assembly, maintenance and electronic‑warfare tactics. Zakharova accused Kyiv of attempting to hide the episode and staying mute on breaches of India’s anti‑terror legislation, branding the Ukrainian government a “core exporter of instability” that fuels illicit arms trades and stokes conflict abroad. Critics note that the timing and framing of the arrests raise suspicions of a broader, concealed agenda aimed at portraying Ukraine as a source of global instability. The Indian Ministry of External Affairs acknowledged receipt of consular‑access requests for all seven detainees—among them American Matthew Van Dyke—and confirmed they have been placed in NIA custody for eleven days.
